examples_and_documentation.overview: Complete examples and documentation templates for the ABC Bank modernization project. This includes README structure, API documentation, and complete working examples.

readme_template.description: Comprehensive README.md for the modernized application
readme_template.template:
# ABC Bank - Modernized Banking Application
A modernized version of the legacy ABC Bank application, transformed from Struts 1.x/JSP/SQLite to Spring Boot 3.x/PostgreSQL with cloud-native deployment on Kubernetes/OpenShift.
## 📋 Table of Contents
- [Overview](#overview) - [Architecture](#architecture) - [Technology Stack](#technology-stack) - [Prerequisites](#prerequisites) - [Getting Started](#getting-started) - [Building the Application](#building-the-application) - [Running Locally](#running-locally) - [Deploying to OpenShift](#deploying-to-openshift) - [API Documentation](#api-documentation) - [Demo Credentials](#demo-credentials) - [Known Limitations](#known-limitations) - [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
## 🎯 Overview
ABC Bank is a demonstration of legacy application modernization, showcasing the transformation of a traditional Java web application into a modern, cloud-native microservice.
### Key Features
- ✅ User authentication and authorization - ✅ Account management and summary - ✅ Transaction history viewing - ✅ Fund transfers between accounts - ✅ RESTful API design - ✅ Cloud-native architecture - ✅ Kubernetes/OpenShift deployment
### Modernization Highlights
| Legacy | Modern | |--------|--------| | Struts 1.x | Spring Boot 3.x | | JSP/Servlets | Spring MVC / Thymeleaf | | JDBC | Spring Data JPA | | SQLite | PostgreSQL | | WAR deployment | Containerized (Docker) | | Manual deployment | Helm charts |
## 🏗️ Architecture
``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ OpenShift/Kubernetes │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ABC Bank │◄──────────────────►│ PostgreSQL │ │ │ │ (Spring │ JDBC Connection │ Database │ │ │ │ Boot) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ └──────┬───────┘ └──────────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ Exposed via │ │ │ OpenShift Route │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │ Route │ │ │ │ (HTTPS) │ │ │ └──────────────┘ │ └───────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ ▼ External Users ```
### Component Architecture
``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Spring Boot Application │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Controllers │───►│ Services │───►│Repositories │ │ │ │ (REST/MVC) │ │ (Business │ │ (JPA) │ │ │ │ │ │ Logic) │ │ │ │ │ └─────────────┘ └─────────────┘ └──────┬──────┘ │ │ │ │ │ ┌─────────────┐ ┌─────────────┐ │ │ │ │ Spring │ │ Spring │ │ │ │ │ Security │ │ Actuator │ │ │ │ └─────────────┘ └─────────────┘ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┘ │ ▼ ┌──────────────┐ │ PostgreSQL │ │ Database │ └──────────────┘ ```
## 🛠️ Technology Stack
### Backend - **Java**: 17 - **Framework**: Spring Boot 3.2.0 - **Build Tool**: Maven 3.9+ - **Database**: PostgreSQL 15 - **ORM**: Spring Data JPA / Hibernate - **Security**: Spring Security with BCrypt - **Monitoring**: Spring Boot Actuator
### Frontend - **Option A**: Thymeleaf (Server-side rendering) - **Option B**: React 18 / Next.js 14 (SPA)
### DevOps - **Containerization**: Docker - **Orchestration**: Kubernetes / OpenShift - **Deployment**: Helm 3 - **CI/CD**: (To be configured)
## 📦 Prerequisites
### For Local Development - Java 17 or higher - Maven 3.9 or higher - Docker Desktop - PostgreSQL 15 (or use Docker) - Git
### For OpenShift Deployment - OpenShift CLI (`oc`) - Helm 3 - Access to OpenShift cluster - Docker registry access
## 🚀 Getting Started
### 1. Clone the Repository
```bash git clone https://github.com/abcbank/abc-bank-modernized.git cd abc-bank-modernized ```
### 2. Set Up Local PostgreSQL
#### Option A: Using Docker ```bash docker run -d \ --name abc-bank-postgres \ -e POSTGRES_DB=abcbank \ -e POSTGRES_USER=abcbank \ -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=changeme \ -p 5432:5432 \ postgres:15-alpine ```
#### Option B: Local Installation ```bash # Create database createdb abcbank
# Create user psql -c "CREATE USER abcbank WITH PASSWORD 'changeme';" psql -c "GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE abcbank TO abcbank;" ```
## 🔨 Building the Application
### Build with Maven
```bash cd backend mvn clean package ```
This will: - Compile the Java code - Run unit tests - Create a JAR file in `target/abc-bank-2.0.0.jar`
### Build Docker Image
```bash cd backend docker build -t abc-bank-backend:latest . ```
### Verify Build
```bash # Check JAR file ls -lh target/*.jar
# Check Docker image docker images | grep abc-bank ```
## 💻 Running Locally
### Using Maven
```bash cd backend mvn spring-boot:run ```
### Using JAR
```bash cd backend java -jar target/abc-bank-2.0.0.jar ```
### Using Docker
```bash # Run PostgreSQL docker run -d --name postgres \ -e POSTGRES_DB=abcbank \ -e POSTGRES_USER=abcbank \ -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=changeme \ postgres:15-alpine
# Run application docker run -d --name abc-bank \ --link postgres:postgres \ -e SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/abcbank \ -e SPRING_DATASOURCE_USERNAME=abcbank \ -e SPRING_DATASOURCE_PASSWORD=changeme \ -p 8080:8080 \ abc-bank-backend:latest ```
### Access the Application
- **Web UI**: http://localhost:8080 - **Health Check**: http://localhost:8080/actuator/health - **API Base**: http://localhost:8080/api
## ☸️ Deploying to OpenShift
### 1. Login to OpenShift
```bash oc login https://api.your-cluster.com:6443 oc new-project abc-bank ```
### 2. Build and Push Docker Image
```bash # Tag image for registry docker tag abc-bank-backend:latest your-registry/abc-bank-backend:latest
# Push to registry docker push your-registry/abc-bank-backend:latest ```
### 3. Update Helm Values
Edit `helm/abc-bank/values.yaml`:
```yaml app: image: repository: your-registry/abc-bank-backend tag: latest
database: password: your-secure-password # Change this!
route: host: abc-bank.apps.your-cluster.com ```
### 4. Install with Helm
```bash # Validate chart helm lint helm/abc-bank
# Dry run helm install abc-bank helm/abc-bank --dry-run --debug
# Install helm install abc-bank helm/abc-bank ```
### 5. Monitor Deployment
```bash # Watch pods kubectl get pods -w
# Check logs kubectl logs -f deployment/abc-bank kubectl logs -f deployment/abc-bank-postgres
# Get route oc get route abc-bank ```
### 6. Access Application
```bash export ROUTE_HOST=$(oc get route abc-bank -o jsonpath='{.spec.host}') echo "Application URL: https://$ROUTE_HOST" open https://$ROUTE_HOST ```
## 📚 API Documentation
### Authentication
#### Login ```http POST /api/auth/login Content-Type: application/json
{ "username": "john.doe", "password": "password" } ```
#### Logout ```http POST /api/auth/logout ```
### Accounts
#### Get User Accounts ```http GET /api/accounts Authorization: Bearer {token} ```
Response: ```json [ { "id": 1, "accountNumber": "ACC1001", "accountType": "SAVINGS", "balance": 5000.00, "ownerName": "John Doe" } ] ```
#### Get Account Details ```http GET /api/accounts/{id} Authorization: Bearer {token} ```
### Transactions
#### Get Transaction History ```http GET /api/transactions?accountId={accountId} Authorization: Bearer {token} ```
Response: ```json [ { "id": 1, "amount": -100.00, "type": "DEBIT", "description": "ATM Withdrawal", "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00", "balanceAfter": 4900.00 } ] ```
### Transfers
#### Execute Fund Transfer ```http POST /api/transfers Authorization: Bearer {token} Content-Type: application/json
{ "fromAccount": "ACC1001", "toAccount": "ACC2001", "amount": 100.00, "description": "Payment" } ```
Response: ```json { "success": true, "message": "Transfer successful", "referenceNumber": "TXN123456" } ```
## 🔑 Demo Credentials
### Users
| Username | Password | Role | Accounts | |----------|----------|------|----------| | john.doe | password | USER | ACC1001, ACC1002 | | jane.smith | password | USER | ACC2001, ACC2002, ACC2003 | | admin | password | ADMIN | - |
### Sample Accounts
| Account Number | Type | Balance | Owner | |----------------|------|---------|-------| | ACC1001 | SAVINGS | $5,000.00 | John Doe | | ACC1002 | CHECKING | $2,500.00 | John Doe | | ACC2001 | SAVINGS | $10,000.00 | Jane Smith | | ACC2002 | CHECKING | $3,500.00 | Jane Smith | | ACC2003 | FIXED_DEPOSIT | $50,000.00 | Jane Smith |
## ⚠️ Known Limitations
### Database Storage - **PostgreSQL uses emptyDir volume** - Data is ephemeral and will be lost when the pod restarts - This is intentional for demo purposes (no PersistentVolumeClaims allowed) - Data is automatically reseeded from `data.sql` on startup
### Scalability - **Single replica deployment** - Not configured for horizontal scaling - **No load balancing** - Single instance only - **No database replication** - Single PostgreSQL instance
### Security - **Demo credentials** - Hardcoded in `data.sql` - **Simple authentication** - Form-based login only - **No OAuth/OIDC** - Basic Spring Security implementation
### Production Readiness - **Not production-ready** - This is a demonstration/proof-of-concept - **No backup/restore** - Data loss is acceptable - **No monitoring/alerting** - Basic Actuator endpoints only - **No CI/CD pipeline** - Manual build and deployment
## 🔧 Troubleshooting
### Application Won't Start
**Problem**: Application fails to start with database connection errors
**Solution**: ```bash # Check PostgreSQL is running kubectl get pods | grep postgres
# Check database credentials kubectl get secret abc-bank-db-secret -o yaml
# View application logs kubectl logs deployment/abc-bank ```
### Health Checks Failing
**Problem**: Readiness/liveness probes failing
**Solution**: ```bash # Check health endpoint manually kubectl exec deployment/abc-bank -- curl localhost:8080/actuator/health
# Increase initialDelaySeconds in values.yaml app: readinessProbe: initialDelaySeconds: 60 # Increase if needed ```
### Data Not Persisting
**Problem**: Data disappears after pod restart
**Solution**: This is expected behavior with emptyDir volumes. Data is reseeded from `data.sql` on startup.
### Cannot Access Route
**Problem**: Route returns 503 or connection refused
**Solution**: ```bash # Check route exists oc get route abc-bank
# Check service endpoints kubectl get endpoints abc-bank
# Verify pods are ready kubectl get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=abc-bank ```
### Build Failures
**Problem**: Maven build fails
**Solution**: ```bash # Clean and rebuild mvn clean install -U
# Skip tests if needed mvn clean package -DskipTests
# Check Java version java -version # Should be 17+ ```
## 📞 Support
For issues and questions: - **GitHub Issues**: https://github.com/abcbank/abc-bank-modernized/issues - **Email**: devops@abcbank.com - **Documentation**: https://docs.abcbank.com
## 📄 License
Copyright © 2024 ABC Bank. All rights reserved.
This is a demonstration project for educational purposes.
---
**Built with ❤️ by the ABC Bank DevOps Team**

complete_example_project.description: Complete example showing all components working together

category.name: Configuration
category.item: No hardcoded passwords or API keys

success_criteria.criterion: All legacy features work identically in modernized version
Code follows Spring Boot best practices
